meta.currentPhase: MVP — get a working app first
meta.projectDescription: Web app that transcribes audio from URLs using yt-dlp + faster-whisper (Python serverless API) with a SvelteKit/TypeScript frontend, deployed to Vercel.

techStack.framework: Tauri 2 desktop shell + SvelteKit UI + bundled Python sidecar

knownDecisions.decision: Stopped gitignoring _memory/ and added AGENTS.md + __handovers__/CURRENT.md as the cross-tool (Claude Code / Copilot / OpenCode) session continuity mechanism.
knownDecisions.rationale: _memory/ was gitignored by RNA's installer default, so it never left the machine it was written on and couldn't function as shared state across tools or clones. __handovers__/ existed but was empty and unused.

knownDecisions.decision: Fixed 'sidecar binary missing' in src-tauri/src/lib.rs sidecar_path() — TWO distinct path bugs. (1) Inner binary name: code joined the resource-dir name onto itself (…/transcribe-sidecar-aarch64-apple-darwin) but PyInstaller names the inner binary just 'transcribe-sidecar'; added SIDECAR_BINARY_NAME const. (2) Missing 'binaries/' prefix: SIDECAR_RESOURCE_DIR omitted the binaries/ prefix, so resolve(Resource) pointed at target/debug/transcribe-sidecar-…/ but Tauri preserves the bundle.resources source structure and actually places it at target/debug/binaries/transcribe-sidecar-…/; changed the const to 'binaries/transcribe-sidecar-aarch64-apple-darwin'.
knownDecisions.rationale: Tauri's bundle.resources preserves source dir structure (incl. the binaries/ prefix) when copying resources, and PyInstaller's --name (not the dir name) is the inner binary. cargo check passes; resolved dev path verified present + executable. Full UI smoke still pending (port 5173 was held by a pre-existing dev instance during verification).

knownDecisions.decision: Fixed Instagram reel-URL misclassification in api/transcribe_core.py _is_instagram_profile_url(): it only checked segments[0] for a post keyword (reel/p/tv/stories), so username-prefixed reel URLs (instagram.com/<user>/reel/<id> — IG's current share-link format) were wrongly classified as profile pages and blocked with IG_PROFILE_UNSUPPORTED. Changed to scan all segments except the last for a post keyword, since an individual post always has the keyword immediately followed by an id segment regardless of username prefix.
knownDecisions.rationale: Verified against 12 enumerated URL shapes (profile, reels-tab, bare/username-prefixed reel/p/tv/stories) — all classify correctly, including the user's exact reported URL. Live-tested the downstream oEmbed call against the real URL and got a successful response, confirming the full probe pipeline (not just the classifier) works. No pytest harness exists for api/ in this repo — verification used ad-hoc python3 -c scripts; flagged as a backlog gap in __handovers__/CURRENT.md.

knownDecisions.decision: F14 Progressive Picker + Probe Cache + Visible Activity. Three coordinated changes addressing 'picker takes too long, no UI feedback, re-paste is slow' for slow URLs like https://www.youtube.com/@MillieAdrian/shorts: (1) Python sidecar INITIAL_PAGE_SIZE 20→5 (extract_flat='in_playlist' over a 100+ Shorts channel took 5–10s before; now ~1.5s for the user's URL), with new process_info callback emitting per-entry events that yt-dlp never fires for in_playlist mode (kept as best-effort). (2) New probe_cache SQLite table (TTL 15 min) — re-paste same URL is instant; invalidated on 'Transcribe X videos' click. (3) New Tauri 'probe-activity' event channel that streams sidecar stdout to the UI; replaces eprintln!-only feedback with a live ProbeActivityStrip (mono 10.5px, severity color, Refresh link). Frontend listens for entry events and appends rows live (works when yt-dlp exposes them; falls back to the final-result merge path otherwise).
knownDecisions.rationale: Verified standalone: full probe for the user's URL completes in 1.465s end-to-end (was: 5–10s of waiting with only terminal output). Rust unit tests 14/14 pass (4 new probe_cache tests + 10 existing, schema test confirms the new probe_cache table is created alongside settings/history/jobs/job_items). svelte-check found 0 errors in the new ProbeActivityStrip.svelte and UrlInputPanel.svelte F14 additions. Not yet verified in the running app (npm run tauri:dev not started this session). Spec at __specs__/F14-progressive-picker-cache.md.

knownDecisions.decision: F14 UX polish (Phase 2): addressed two user complaints — (a) activity strip showed 'Probe ready' without context, clobbering the more informative 'N videos · ready · cached Xm ago' message that handleProbeResult pushes from the final probe result; (b) in picker mode, the URL input + activity strip pushed the VideoPicker + action bar off-screen for short left-pane heights. Fix 1: removed the redundant 'Probe ready' push in handleProbeActivityEvent's done branch — the Rust bridge delivers event:done to the webview before the probe_url invoke resolves, so the listener ran first and stomped the better message. Fix 2: split UrlInputPanel template into pickerMode / non-picker branches; in picker mode, render a compact URL input at the top (.picker-url-line, flex-shrink:0) and wrap VideoPicker in .picker-scroll (flex:1, overflow:hidden) so it scrolls inside its own height while the action bar stays pinned to the parent flex column's bottom.
knownDecisions.rationale: Verified by svelte-check (no new errors), cargo check (no Rust changes), and re-verified standalone probe timing 1.465s. Not yet verified in the running app — visual confirmation deferred to next session. Files: src/lib/desktop/UrlInputPanel.svelte (template restructure + 2 new CSS classes), __handovers__/CURRENT.md session 4 entry.

knownDecisions.decision: Set up the dirvine/tauri-mcp MCP server (https://mcpservers.org/servers/dirvine/tauri-mcp) for this project, available to both Claude Code and GitHub Copilot. Built and installed the Rust binary via `cargo install --path .` from a shallow clone (now on PATH at ~/.cargo/bin/tauri-mcp, v0.1.5). Added root `.mcp.json` (mcpServers key — read by Claude Code and Copilot CLI) and `.vscode/mcp.json` (servers key, type: stdio — read by Copilot in VS Code), both invoking `tauri-mcp serve`. Despite the host/port-looking args, `serve` is pure JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio (confirmed by reading src/server.rs and a live initialize handshake test) — the README's Claude-Desktop-only DXT/Node-wrapper workaround does not apply to Claude Code or Copilot, which both speak stdio MCP natively.
knownDecisions.rationale: User wants AI-assisted Tauri app testing/debugging (process launch, screenshots, input simulation, JS execution, IPC calls) available from any coding agent on this repo, not just one tool. Used `command: "tauri-mcp"` (relying on PATH) rather than hardcoding an absolute machine-specific path, since the binary needs to be cargo-installed by each contributor anyway and an absolute path would break for any other team member or env. Verified end-to-end with a manual MCP initialize handshake before considering this done.

knownDecisions.decision: Built first beta DMG (npm run tauri:build, target/release/bundle/dmg/Transcribe_0.1.0_aarch64.dmg, aarch64 only, unsigned). DMG creation initially failed (-1743 Apple Events / Automation permission for the VS Code helper process to control Finder during create-dmg's AppleScript styling step) — fixed by granting Automation access in System Settings, one-time per machine. During UAT on this DMG, found and root-caused 3 distinct bugs (logged as __specs__/FIX-08, FIX-09, FIX-10) plus confirmed a known gap (FFmpeg CLI not bundled — opened openspec/changes/bundle-ffmpeg-sidecar/, proposal+design+specs+tasks all written, not yet implemented).
knownDecisions.rationale: FIX-08 (Instagram /reels/<id>/ plural oEmbed 400) and FIX-09 (Safari cookie PermissionError surfacing as generic INTERNAL during yt-dlp's lazy re-read, one layer past the existing validation in _inject_ig_browser_cookies) were both reproduced directly with standalone python3 scripts — root cause confirmed, not guessed, per this repo's systematic-debugging discipline. FIX-10 (YouTube /shorts tab 'invalid literal for int() with base 10: ' crash from a UAT screenshot) could NOT be reproduced locally (probe_url's exact ydl_opts ran clean against the same URL; no log/crash-log/app.db evidence on this machine matches it) — explicitly left unimplemented and flagged 'needs reproduction' rather than shipping a speculative fix, since guessing here would violate the Iron Law (no fixes without root cause). FFmpeg bundling was scoped as an OpenSpec change (not a __specs__ single-file fix) because it spans build-script, sidecar resolution logic, and yt-dlp ffmpeg_location wiring across multiple files with a real design tradeoff (which static-build source to pin).

knownDecisions.decision: Implemented and shipped openspec/changes/bundle-ffmpeg-sidecar/ (22/25 tasks done; remaining 3 are this close-out step). Bundled static ffmpeg/ffprobe (evermeet.cx 8.1.2, x86_64 via Rosetta 2 — evermeet.cx has no native arm64 build) into the PyInstaller sidecar build via new scripts/fetch_ffmpeg.py, wired into scripts/build_sidecar.py's build(). Replaced api/sidecar.py's check_ffmpeg() with resolve_ffmpeg() (bundled path first when frozen, PATH fallback in dev mode) and threaded the resolved path into api/transcribe_core.py's download_audio() via a new ffmpeg_location parameter set on yt-dlp's ydl_opts. Added the first pytest harness for api/ (api/__tests__/, 5 tests). Rebuilt and published as GitHub release v0.1.1 (DMG ~159MB, up from ~100MB) plus a matching Notion 'App Versions' row, superseding v0.1.0 which still required `brew install ffmpeg`.
knownDecisions.rationale: User hit the exact FFMPEG_MISSING error on the v0.1.0 DMG during their own testing, making this no longer hypothetical — implemented the already-written OpenSpec change rather than just telling the user to `brew install ffmpeg` locally. Picked evermeet.cx over Homebrew-copy or a first-run installer-wizard alternative (explicitly considered and rejected — reintroduces the 'needs network on first run' problem the whole change exists to avoid) after explicit user approval to download+execute a third-party binary (correctly blocked by the auto-mode classifier on the first attempt — approval was sought and given before retrying). Verified END-TO-END, not just unit-tested: ran the real frozen transcribe-sidecar binary directly with PATH stripped to /usr/bin:/bin (no Homebrew anywhere) and successfully downloaded+transcoded a real YouTube video to a valid mp3 (confirmed via ffprobe) — proves the bundled binary is actually used, not just present on disk. Did NOT implement FIX-08/FIX-09/FIX-10 in this session — those remain open, tracked separately in __specs__/INDEX.md.

knownDecisions.decision: Root-caused and fixed FIX-10 (previously blocked, marked 'needs reproduction'). User reported v0.1.1 still crashing with `invalid literal for int() with base 10: ''` on an M2 Mac, on every URL type, not just /shorts. Got the real Python traceback by having the user run `/Applications/Transcribe.app/Contents/MacOS/transcribe` directly from Terminal (the Rust-side `sidecar.log` at `app_log_dir()` — NOT `app_data_dir()`, an earlier wrong guess this session — never got created, so file-based logging was a dead end; live stderr capture was the only path that worked). Root cause: `platform.mac_ver()[0]` returns a malformed version string on some macOS versions (confirmed empty/blank dot-segment), and yt-dlp's plugin discovery (`yt_dlp/update.py:_get_variant_and_executable_path`, called unconditionally on every `YoutubeDL()` construction) calls `version_tuple()` on it WITHOUT yt-dlp's own `lenient=True` option — crashing on literally every probe/download attempt, explaining why it hit every URL type. Fixed by patching `platform.mac_ver()` in `api/sidecar.py` before `yt_dlp` is imported, normalizing any malformed release string. Verified by simulating the exact broken `mac_ver()` output and confirming `YoutubeDL()` now constructs successfully (it previously raised). Rebuilt the sidecar + DMG and overwrote the existing v0.1.1 GitHub release asset and Notion entry in place (did not cut a new version tag, per explicit user instruction).
knownDecisions.rationale: This is the textbook case the systematic-debugging skill's Iron Law exists for — last session correctly refused to guess a fix without evidence, and this session got the evidence first (the real traceback) before touching any code. The fix targets yt-dlp's bug (a known half-fix already exists in their own `version_tuple(lenient=True)` API, just not wired through this one call site) without needing to patch yt-dlp itself, which would break on every yt-dlp version upgrade. Patch point chosen deliberately at sidecar.py's top, before `import yt_dlp`, to be defensive regardless of which code path constructs the first `YoutubeDL()` instance (confirmed two different call sites hit this — probe's oEmbed-bypass path for /shorts, and run_download's path for every other URL — both converge on the same crash for the same reason).

openQuestions: Project pivoted from SvelteKit+Vercel-serverless to Tauri desktop + bundled sidecar at some point after RNA setup (2026-05-29) — .rna/rna-schema.json was never regenerated to reflect this. Worth re-running the RNA adapter, or just treating AGENTS.md/README.md as the corrected source of truth going forward?
__specs__/INDEX.md references transcribe-handoff.md as the architecture reference doc, but that file does not exist in the repo. Lost, renamed, or never committed?
src-tauri/Cargo.toml's version still says 0.1.0 while tauri.conf.json now says 0.1.1 — cosmetic mismatch, doesn't affect the bundle filename, but worth reconciling next time either is touched.

evermeet.cx's ffmpeg/ffprobe are x86_64-only (run via Rosetta 2) inside an otherwise-native aarch64 sidecar bundle — works fine today, but if a native arm64 static ffmpeg source is ever found, swapping it in would remove the one non-native binary in the bundle.

Waiting on the M2 user to confirm the overwritten v0.1.1 DMG actually resolves the crash on their machine — fix is verified via simulated reproduction of the exact broken mac_ver() output, but not yet confirmed against the real affected hardware/OS.
